children.label: A
children.text: the removal of lead-based paint and lead-contaminated dust, the permanent containment or encapsulation of lead-based paint, the replacement of lead-painted surfaces or fixtures, and the removal or covering of lead contaminated soil; and

children.label: B
children.text: all preparation, cleanup, disposal, and postabatement clearance testing activities associated with such measures.

children.heading: Accessible surface
children.label: 2 Accessible surface
children.text: The term “accessible surface” means an interior or exterior surface painted with lead-based paint that is accessible for a young child to mouth or chew.

children.label: A
children.text: a contractor, inspector, or supervisor who has completed a training program certified by the appropriate Federal agency and has met any other requirements for certification or licensure established by such agency or who has been certified by any State through a program which has been found by such Federal agency to be at least as rigorous as the Federal certification program; and

children.label: B
children.text: workers or designers who have fully met training requirements established by the appropriate Federal agency.

children.heading: Contract for the purchase and sale of residential real property
children.label: 4 Contract for the purchase and sale of residential real property
children.text: The term “contract for the purchase and sale of residential real property” means any contract or agreement in which one party agrees to purchase an interest in real property on which there is situated 1 or more residential dwellings used or occupied, or intended to be used or occupied, in whole or in part, as the home or residence of 1 or more persons.

children.heading: Deteriorated paint
children.label: 5 Deteriorated paint
children.text: The term “deteriorated paint” means any interior or exterior paint that is peeling, chipping, chalking or cracking or any paint located on an interior or exterior surface or fixture that is damaged or deteriorated.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 6 Evaluation
children.text: The term “evaluation” means risk assessment, inspection, or risk assessment and inspection.

children.label: A
children.text: section 1715(d)(3) or 1715z–1 of title 12;l

children.label: B
children.text: section 1 of the Housing and Urban Development Act of 1965;

children.label: C
children.text: ; orsection 1437f of this title

children.label: D
children.text: sections 1472(a), 1474, 1484, 1485, 1486 and 1490m of this title.

children.heading: Federally owned housing
children.label: 8 Federally owned housing
children.text: The term “federally owned housing” means residential dwellings owned or managed by a Federal agency, or for which a Federal agency is a trustee or conservator. For the purpose of this paragraph, the term “Federal agency” includes the Department of Housing and Urban Development, the Farmers Home Administration, the Resolution Trust Corporation, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, the General Services Administration, the Department of Defense, the Department of Veterans Affairs, the Department of the Interior, the Department of Transportation, and any other Federal agency.

children.heading: Federally supported work
children.label: 9 Federally supported work
children.text: The term “federally supported work” means any lead hazard evaluation or reduction activities conducted in federally owned or assisted housing or funded in whole or in part through any financial assistance program of the Department of Housing and Urban Development, the Farmers Home Administration, or the Department of Veterans Affairs.

children.heading: Friction surface
children.label: 10 Friction surface
children.text: The term “friction surface” means an interior or exterior surface that is subject to abrasion or friction, including certain window, floor, and stair surfaces.

children.heading: Impact surface
children.label: 11 Impact surface
children.text: The term “impact surface” means an interior or exterior surface that is subject to damage by repeated impacts, for example, certain parts of door frames.

children.heading: Inspection
children.label: 12 Inspection
children.text: The term “inspection” means a surface-by-surface investigation to determine the presence of lead-based paint as provided in and the provision of a report explaining the results of the investigation.section 4822(c) of this title

children.heading: Interim controls
children.label: 13 Interim controls
children.text: The term “interim controls” means a set of measures designed to reduce temporarily human exposure or likely exposure to lead-based paint hazards, including specialized cleaning, repairs, maintenance, painting, temporary containment, ongoing monitoring of lead-based paint hazards or potential hazards, and the establishment and operation of management and resident education programs.

children.heading: Lead-based paint
children.label: 14 Lead-based paint
children.text: The term “lead-based paint” means paint or other surface coatings that contain lead in excess of limits established under .section 4822(c) of this title

children.heading: Lead-based paint hazard
children.label: 15 Lead-based paint hazard
children.text: The term “lead-based paint hazard” means any condition that causes exposure to lead from lead-contaminated dust, lead-contaminated soil, lead-contaminated paint that is deteriorated or present in accessible surfaces, friction surfaces, or impact surfaces that would result in adverse human health effects as established by the appropriate Federal agency.

children.heading: Lead-contaminated dust
children.label: 16 Lead-contaminated dust
children.text: The term “lead-contaminated dust” means surface dust in residential dwellings that contains an area or mass concentration of lead in excess of levels determined by the appropriate Federal agency to pose a threat of adverse health effects in pregnant women or young children.

children.heading: Lead-contaminated soil
children.label: 17 Lead-contaminated soil
children.text: The term “lead-contaminated soil” means bare soil on residential real property that contains lead at or in excess of the levels determined to be hazardous to human health by the appropriate Federal agency.

children.label: A
children.text: is secured by a first lien on any interest in residential real property; and

children.label: i
children.text: is insured, guaranteed, made, or assisted by the Department of Housing and Urban Development, the Department of Veterans Affairs, or the Farmers Home Administration, or by any other agency of the Federal Government; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is intended to be sold by each originating mortgage institution to any federally chartered secondary mortgage market institution.

children.heading: Originating mortgage institution
children.label: 19 Originating mortgage institution
children.text: The term “originating mortgage institution” means a lender that provides mortgage loans.

children.heading: Priority housing
children.label: 20 Priority housing
children.text: The term “priority housing” means target housing that qualifies as affordable housing under , including housing that receives assistance under subsection (b) or () of .section 12745 of this titlesection 1437f of this titleo

children.heading: Public housing
children.label: 21 Public housing
children.text: The term “public housing” has the same meaning given the term in .section 1437a(b) of this title

children.heading: Reduction
children.label: 22 Reduction
children.text: The term “reduction” means measures designed to reduce or eliminate human exposure to lead-based paint hazards through methods including interim controls and abatement.

children.label: A
children.text: a single-family dwelling, including attached structures such as porches and stoops; or

children.label: B
children.text: a single-family dwelling unit in a structure that contains more than 1 separate residential dwelling unit, and in which each such unit is used or occupied, or intended to be used or occupied, in whole or in part, as the home or residence of 1 or more persons.

children.heading: Residential real property
children.label: 24 Residential real property
children.text: The term “residential real property” means real property on which there is situated 1 or more residential dwellings used or occupied, or intended to be used or occupied, in whole or in part, as the home or residence of 1 or more persons.

children.label: A
children.text: information gathering regarding the age and history of the housing and occupancy by children under age 6;

children.label: B
children.text: visual inspection;

children.label: C
children.text: limited wipe sampling or other environmental sampling techniques;

children.label: D
children.text: other activity as may be appropriate; and

children.label: E
children.text: provision of a report explaining the results of the investigation.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 26 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.heading: Target housing
children.label: 27 Target housing
children.text: The term “target housing” means any housing constructed prior to 1978, except housing for the elderly or persons with disabilities or any 0-bedroom dwelling (unless any child who is less than 6 years of age resides or is expected to reside in such housing). In the case of jurisdictions which banned the sale or use of lead-based paint prior to 1978, the Secretary, at the Secretary’s discretion, may designate an earlier date.
